introSection: Batwing is a steel flying roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened on June 16, 2001, it was one of three Flying Dutchman installations from Vekoma. The ride is located towards the back of the park next to Superman: Ride of Steel. Batwing is a clone of the defunct Firehawk at Kings Island.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Batwing opened on June 16, 2001 as the first flying roller coaster on the East Coast.

Batwing last operated on July 7, 2025. In September 2025, a sign appeared outside the coaster claiming that it has permanently closed.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: At the start of the ride, riders are put at a face-up position. When the trains depart the station, they turn in the opposite direction. After this, the train makes a left turn and climbs the tall chain lift hill. During the lift hill, riders can see Superman: Ride of Steel, Joker's Jinx, Wonder Woman Lasso of Truth, Penguin's Blizzard River and other rides. At the top, the train switches to a flying position and dives at. Then, the train travels through a horseshoe. After this, riders switch to a face-up position, make a right turn and enter a tall vertical loop. The train then switches to flying position and travels through some turns. Riders hit a double in-line twist, followed by a helix. After the helix, riders are switched to a face-up position as the train hits the final brake run.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Batwing has a yellow track and purple supports.

fullText:
Batwing is a steel flying roller coaster currently standing but not operating at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened on June 16, 2001, it was one of three Flying Dutchman installations from Vekoma. The ride is located towards the back of the park next to Superman: Ride of Steel. Batwing is a clone of the defunct Firehawk at Kings Island.

Batwing opened on June 16, 2001 as the first flying roller coaster on the East Coast.

Batwing last operated on July 7, 2025. In September 2025, a sign appeared outside the coaster claiming that it has permanently closed.



At the start of the ride, riders are put at a face-up position. When the trains depart the station, they turn in the opposite direction. After this, the train makes a left turn and climbs the tall chain lift hill. During the lift hill, riders can see Superman: Ride of Steel, Joker's Jinx, Wonder Woman Lasso of Truth, Penguin's Blizzard River and other rides. At the top, the train switches to a flying position and dives at. Then, the train travels through a horseshoe. After this, riders switch to a face-up position, make a right turn and enter a tall vertical loop. The train then switches to flying position and travels through some turns. Riders hit a double in-line twist, followed by a helix. After the helix, riders are switched to a face-up position as the train hits the final brake run.

Batwing has a yellow track and purple supports.
